extraction.one_line_summary: HAYTER Ethan won a selective sprint after INEOS paced hard enough to drop VERNON Ethan but preserve sprint leadout through SHEFFIELD Magnus's massive turn.
tactical_patterns: INEOS via DE PLUS Laurens paced climbs hard enough to shed pure sprinters like VERNON but keep GC riders engaged, optimizing HAYTER's winning odds.
SHEFFIELD Magnus's exceptionally long leadout opened HAYTER's sprint very early, suggesting INEOS prioritized track position over pure timing in selective bunch sprints.
UAE with POLANC Jan shared peloton control despite no sprinter, revealing willingness to burn domestiques maintaining GC positioning for classification riders.
VLASOV Aleksandr finishing fourth shows BORA positioning GC leader in sprint chaos rather than sitting behind, perhaps testing form or bonus seconds strategy.

rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: positive: First World Tour road win demonstrates punch survives harder selections than pure sprints
rider_signals.SHEFFIELD Magnus: positive: Leadout power exceptional enough to merit commentary superlatives, domestique positioning precision unusually high
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: JUMBO controlling race for TT leader suggests team confidence holding GC through road stages
rider_signals.DE PLUS Laurens: positive: Infernal tempo setting thins peloton to 70 riders, pivotal to INEOS sprint setup
rider_signals.VERNON Ethan: negative: Dropped on long drags reveals pure sprinters unable to contest selective finishes here
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: neutral: Best GC finisher in sprint shows positioning commitment but unclear if tactical priority
rider_signals.POLANC Jan: neutral: Long pulls for UAE without sprinter suggests domestique role supporting GC defense rather than stage hunting
